mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as bond yields rise and equity valuations contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns weakens below -0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in >50% of cases, or their predictive power collapses (Rank IC < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals tight labor markets, prompting inflationary pressures that force central bank rate hikes, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%, or the negative correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns weakens below -0.30.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving consumer confidence and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral demand spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 5d returns in >50% of instances with rising HOUST, or the predictive power of HOUST on SPX returns weakens (Rank IC < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k signals sustained employment expansion, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or the positive correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns weakens below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling real rates (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL) signals monetary policy misalignment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors price in higher volatility.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60d SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the combined signal's Rank IC falls below -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weak manufacturing activity, reducing corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >50% of instances with declining INDPRO, or the negative correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns weakens below -0.20.
